caption:
1:09:35. LONDON - OLYMPIA 

Lungs burning. Legs gone. Still moving.
Representing INDIA 🇮🇳 GHANA 🇬🇭 — two lions in London who refused to slow down.
Last year we gave everything and crossed at 1:14.
This year we came back and took 5 minutes off that.
But I need to tell you something about the night before.
I didn’t sleep much. I dreamed the race. Every station. Every run. Every moment I had mastered it — performed it to its finest.
So when it got real — when the Burpees got us out of breath and the LUNGS were TESTED and our QUADS were giving up — I’d already been there. I’d already come through it.
That’s not magic. That’s work before the work.
Most people only prepare the body.
The ones who win — they’ve already finished the race in their mind a hundred times before race day.
You want to know what separates people?
Not talent. Not time. Not money.
It’s whether you decide — truly decide — what kind of machine you want to be.
Fiat or Ferrari.
We know what we are. 🦁🔥
#Hyrox #London #RaisingLions #NeverGiveUp Visualize TrainToPerform LondonFitness IndiaInLonon

caption:
The moment it becomes a chore  you’ve lost them.
I don’t force my son into anything.
I make it something he wants to run towards.
We race. We laugh. We mess around.
And somewhere in all that fun  he’s building discipline without even realising it.
That’s the secret.
You don’t drill greatness into your kids.
You make it so enjoyable they choose it themselves.
Rain or shine we show up.
But we always show up with a smile.
The lessons that stick aren’t the ones forced on them.
They’re the ones they enjoyed living.
Shape them just enough.
Keep it fun.
Keep it real.
That’s how I’m raising my lion. 🦁
#RaisingLions #FatherAndSon #LegacyOverEverything #leadbyexample

caption:
Happy Mother’s Day 

Behind every picture is a thousand feelings.
Adonis crying. Us laughing. A whole world in a few frames.
Watching my son love his mum the way he does — it does something to you. It mirrors something I’ve felt my whole life but never fully understood until now.
My love for my own mother has grown every single year. The older I get, the more I realise  we are literally a part of them. We came from them. And somewhere in the rush of life, we forget how sacred that is.
There is no love like a mother’s love. Nothing even close.
And to my wife  thank you for being more than comfort. Thank you for pushing me. For making me uncomfortable. For refusing to let me stay small. You don’t just hold this family together  you make us all better.
Happy Mother’s Day to every woman who gives everything, asks for nothing, and loves without limit. 🤍
#MothersDay #Family #RaisingLions #Gratitude #HerLoveIsEverything

caption:
You wouldn’t buy a supercar and keep it in first gear forever.
But that’s exactly how most people treat their bodies.

They move.
They “work out.”
They stay comfortable.

And then wonder why they feel flat, slow, and tired.

If you never rev the engine, what’s the point of having one?

Sprinting isn’t about calories.
It’s a reminder to your body of what it was built for.

Power.
Speed.
Explosiveness.
Life.

Walking keeps you alive.
Jogging keeps you busy.
But intensity wakes you up.

Your body is the vehicle.
Muscle is the engine.
The nervous system is the ignition.

If you’re always cruising, don’t be surprised when nothing changes.

Stop idling.
Drive the machine you were given.

caption:
That moment is pure gold.
Failure → reassurance → courage → execution.
That’s leadership. That’s parenting.

He failed the jump.
I didn’t correct him.
I didn’t shout.
I didn’t rush him.

I reminded him he was safe.
I reminded him he was capable.
I reminded him to trust himself.

Then he jumped.

Confidence isn’t motivation.
It’s a skill built under pressure.
It’s learned when fear shows up and you move anyway.

This is how strong kids are raised.
Not protected from failure.
Guided through it.

This audio carries warrior energy inspired by Rana Kumbha a symbol of fearless strength and royal pride.
“Pralayam” means destruction not chaos, but the kind that destroys weakness and builds power.

Every rep is a reminder:
We break limits.
We rebuild stronger.
We rise like warriors.

caption:
Five months ago, I made a decision.
I wasn’t looking for a new workout.
I was looking for the hardest discipline I could commit to.

I studied almost every sport.
Strength sports.
Endurance sports.
Combat sports.

One kept standing above the rest.

Wrestling.

One of the oldest forms of human training.
Honoured by the ancient Greeks as the ultimate test of the body and mind.
Practiced in India for thousands of years through Kushti and Pehlwani.
Long before gyms existed.
Long before machines.
When strength had to be earned.

Wrestling demands everything.
Your strength.
Your lungs.
Your mobility.
Your balance.
Your awareness.
Your humility.

There is nowhere to hide.
No equipment to blame.
No shortcuts.

That’s exactly why I chose it.

And I chose to walk this path with my son.

Because wrestling doesn’t just build muscle.
It builds humans.

It teaches you how to move your body through space.
How to stay calm under pressure.
How to fall, recover, and rise again.
How to respect your opponent.
How to control power, not abuse it.

For me, this is training.
For him, this is a foundation for life.

This photo isn’t about being the best in the gym.
It’s about choosing discomfort on purpose.
It’s about passing down discipline, awareness, and strength.

Movement is medicine.
Hard things build strong men.
And strong men build strong families.

This is legacy in motion.

caption:
Training and nutrition should feel effortless to execute.
Simple to follow.
Low on decisions.
High on consistency.

When complexity drops, compliance goes up.
That’s how results actually happen.

That’s where I come in.

This is for you if:
You know the basic movements.
You’ve trained before.
You’re tired of guessing.
You want structure, direction, and a clear plan you can execute without thinking.

This is not for you if:
You have no training experience.
You don’t know basic form or technique.
You’re looking for shortcuts without investing in fundamentals.

If you’re a complete beginner, you need a personal trainer near you first.
Learn how to move properly.
Build the foundation.
You cannot skip this step.

Health is not an expense.
It’s an investment.
And your future self will pay you back—with interest.

— FitWithMukund

How you treat the mind
is how life treats you back.

Every outcome starts upstream.
In your thoughts.
In your standards.
In what you allow to repeat daily.

The Stoics understood this.
Great leaders lived by it.

Discipline of the mind creates discipline in life.
Clarity creates power.
Order creates freedom.

Train the mind first.
Everything else follows.

Save this.
Come back to it when life feels noisy.

caption:
Water can heal you.
And water can hurt you.
That’s what nobody tells you.

Your body is over 60% water.
Your brain, blood, muscles, joints, and organs rely on it to function.
But more is not always better.

Drink too little and performance drops fast.
Focus declines.
Strength fades.
Recovery slows.
Fat loss becomes harder.

Drink too much and you create a new problem.
You dilute sodium and electrolytes.
You feel bloated, foggy, weak.
Your nervous system loses balance.

So how much water do you actually need?

Forget “8 glasses a day.”
That rule ignores body size, movement, and sweat.

The real framework is simple.

Start with 30–35 ml per kg of bodyweight per day.
Train hard? Add more.
Sweat a lot? Add electrolytes, not just water.

Your urine doesn’t need to be crystal clear all day.
That’s a sign you may be overdoing it.

The real markers are better energy.
Clear thinking.
Good digestion.
Stable training performance.

Water is the foundation.
But hydration is water plus minerals.

Master this and everything improves.
Get it wrong and you’ll feel “off” no matter how hard you train.

This is why I say:
Before supplements.
Before fancy diets.
Get hydration right.

Most people don’t drink enough water.
Some drink far too much.

Both hurt performance.

Too little water and your brain slows down.
Your energy drops.
Recovery suffers.
Hunger signals get confused.

Too much water and you dilute electrolytes.
You feel bloated, weak, foggy.
Your body loses its natural balance.

So how much do you actually need?

Not a random “8 glasses.”
Not forcing water all day.

The right amount is enough to support your organs, blood volume, and training demands — without flushing minerals out.

Here’s the simple rule most people miss:
Drink to thirst, but anchor it to bodyweight, activity, and sweat loss.

A strong baseline is 30–35 ml per kg of bodyweight per day.
Add more if you train hard.
Add electrolytes if you sweat.

Clear urine all day is not the goal.
Stable energy, focus, digestion, and performance is.

Water is powerful.
Misuse it and it works against you.
Use it correctly and everything improves.

Master the basics.
Hydration is one of them.

caption:
Most people think mobility needs an hour in the gym.
It doesn’t.
It needs movement through the day.

Get up.
Change positions.
Squat.
Rotate.
Balance on one leg.

Your body is designed to move often, not occasionally.
Stiffness is not age.
It’s inactivity.

Five minutes here and there keeps joints healthy.
Keeps balance sharp.
Keeps injuries away.

Train less like a bodybuilder.
Move more like a human.

Why This Combo Works
One trains unilateral control.
One trains bilateral power.
Together they build strength, symmetry, and resilience.
This is how you train hard without breaking your body.

I don’t train just to look good.
I train to live long.
To stay strong.
To play.

caption:
VO₂ Max = The Size of Your Engine.
Not motivation.
Not willpower.
Your engine.

VO₂ max is your body’s ability to take in oxygen, deliver it, and use it under stress.
Bigger VO₂ max = more power, more stamina, faster recovery, sharper focus.

This is how you build it 👇

The Morgan / Norwegian 4×4 Protocol

• 4 minutes hard
• 90–95% max heart rate
• Breathing heavy but controlled

• 4 minutes easy recovery
• Fully moving, not stopping

Repeat 4 rounds.
Total work time: 16 minutes.
Total session: ~30–35 minutes.

Do it 2x per week.

The result?
A stronger heart.
More efficient lungs.
Higher work capacity.
Better performance in training and life.

Most people train muscles.
Elite performers train their engine.

If your engine is weak, everything else caps out.
Build the engine first.

Most people think a fall is just a slip.
But for older adults, it’s often the start of a deadly cascade.

📊 Stats you need to know:
• Over 1 in 4 adults aged 65+ falls every year — that’s millions of seniors losing balance annually. ￼
• Falls are the leading cause of injury and injury-related death in this age group. ￼
• In 2021, about 38,000 older adults died from unintentional falls in the U.S. alone. ￼
• Hip fractures — caused by falls in ~95% of cases — carry a 1-year mortality of ~25% and massive loss of independence. ￼
• Each fall doubles the risk of falling again. ￼

Balance is not optional.
Balance is survival.

Every stumble is a warning signal.
Get stronger. Get steadier. Get ahead of decline — before it hits you or someone you love.

You don’t “find time” for your body.
You either make time now
or you’re forced to later.
Pain doesn’t ask permission.
Discipline does.

caption:
What is VO2 Max?
It’s your body’s ability to use oxygen efficiently during intense exercise.
Not just lung capacity — it’s your full engine.
Heart. Lungs. Muscles.
All working at maximum output.

Higher VO2 Max means:
• Better cardiovascular health
• Faster fat burn
• More daily energy
• Lower disease risk
• Longer, healthier life

It’s one of the strongest markers of longevity.

So how do you improve it fast?

The Norwegian 4×4 Protocol.
Simple. Brutal. Proven.

Here’s how it works:

1️⃣ Warm-Up – 10 minutes
Easy jog or cycle.

2️⃣ 4 minutes HARD
85–95% max heart rate.
Breathing heavy. You can talk, but not comfortably.

3️⃣ 3 minutes EASY
Light jog or slow cycle.
Recover. Control your breathing.

Repeat this 4 times.
Total effort: 16 minutes hard
Total session: 35–40 minutes

Why it works:
• Builds a stronger heart
• Increases mitochondria
• Boosts endurance
• Improves fat-burning
• Raises your biological fitness age

This is why elite Norwegian athletes swear by it.

Start the 4×4 this week.
Your heart will thank you. 🫀🔥

Most people don’t actually need what they think they need.

They need mobility, stability, strength… the foundations that keep you healthy for decades.

If you’re a coach or a client —
What do YOU think matters more?
What they want?
Or what they truly need?

Comment your thoughts below.

How to Stay Injury-Free 🔥

Increase your core temperature before you train.
Don’t go from zero to 100 and shock your system.
Prioritise recovery and rest so your body can adapt.

Most injuries come from cold muscles, poor progression, and no recovery plan.
Fix these and you’ll train harder, longer, and pain-free.

Stay smart. Stay strong. Stay in the game. 💪🔥

WHY IT’S THE #1 PULLING EXERCISE
Because it does exactly what the body was built to do — pull its own weight through space with strength, control, and power.
Weighted pull-ups build the type of physique you can see, feel, and use.
Thick lats. Strong arms. Bulletproof upper back.
Elite strength that carries over to sport, performance, and longevity.

WEIGHTED PULL-UPS — THE KING OF ALL PULLING EXERCISES

1. They build real-world strength.
Pulling your own bodyweight is one thing.
Adding weight forces your body to adapt at a higher level.
This carries over to every movement — rows, deadlifts, grappling, climbing, and overall upper-body power.

2. They build insane back density.
Weighted pull-ups hit the lats, upper back, forearms, biceps, and core in one movement.
No machine will ever match the tension, stability, and muscle recruitment you get from lifting your own body with added load.

3. They improve shoulder health.
Full range, controlled reps strengthen the scapula.
This means better posture, stronger shoulders, and reduced injury risk.

4. They test true athleticism.
Anyone can sit and pull a cable.
Not everyone can strap 10–20–40 kg around their waist and pull themselves up.
It exposes weaknesses and builds elite-level performance.

5. They are the ultimate progression tool.
Beginner: bodyweight pulls.
Intermediate: controlled reps + tempo.
Advanced: weighted reps for strength and power.
You always have room to scale.

6. Grip strength explodes.
Your grip is a predictor of longevity, athleticism, and total-body strength.
Weighted pull-ups force it to grow.

7. Joint-friendly compared to heavy rows.
Less lower back stress.
More lat activation.
Cleaner lines of tension.

Lock in.
Enjoy the time we have.
Build the body, the mind, and the discipline that carries you through life.
Every day is a chance to get better don’t waste it.

caption:
Strength Training
•Goal: Maximize how much weight you can lift (force output).
•Method: Heavy weights, lower reps (1–6).
•Rest Periods: 2–5 minutes between sets.
•Reason: You’re training the nervous system as much as the muscles. Long rest allows full recovery of ATP and nervous system so you can lift maximal loads again.
•Meaning: Strength = moving the weight from point A to point B with optimal form, regardless of how much the muscle “burns.”

Hypertrophy Training
•Goal: Build muscle size (muscle fibers and volume).
•Method: Moderate to heavy weights, moderate reps (6–15+).
•Rest Periods: 30–90 seconds between sets.
•Reason: Shorter rest increases metabolic stress and muscle fatigue, key drivers of muscle growth.
•Meaning: Hypertrophy = focusing on tension and intensity within the muscle. It’s less about how much weight you can move, more about how hard the muscle is working.

✅ In short:
	Strength = Performance of the nervous system. Heavy loads, long rests.
	Hypertrophy = Fatigue of the muscle. Moderate loads, shorter rests.
